annotations.label: Goldbach's conjecture

anchor.snippet: every even natural number greater than 2 is the sum of two prime numbers

annotations.note: Grep shows no Goldbach conjecture statement in Mathlib (the only 'Goldbach' hit is the unrelated Fermat-numbers theorem in NumberTheory/Fermat.lean).

annotations.label: Verified up to 4×10^18

anchor.snippet: The conjecture has been shown to hold for all natural numbers less than

annotations.note: Computational verification bound is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Goldbach's original conjecture (1742)

anchor.snippet: Every integer that can be written as the sum of two primes can also be written as the sum of as many primes

annotations.note: No formalization of Goldbach's 1742 original statement was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Goldbach's marginal conjecture

anchor.snippet: every integer greater than 2 can be written as the sum of three primes

annotations.note: Goldbach's marginal three-primes conjecture is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler's restatement

anchor.snippet: every even integer is a sum of two primes, I regard as a completely certain theorem

annotations.note: Euler's restatement of Goldbach is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Descartes' conjecture

anchor.snippet: Every even number can be expressed as the sum of at most three primes

annotations.note: Descartes' even-as-three-primes conjecture is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chudakov–van der Corput–Estermann (1937–38)

anchor.snippet: almost all even numbers can be written as the sum of two primes

annotations.note: The 'almost all even numbers' result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schnirelmann's theorem (1930)

anchor.snippet: any natural number greater than 1 can be written as the sum of not more than C prime numbers

annotations.note: Schnirelmann density is defined in Combinatorics/Schnirelmann.lean, but the file's TODO explicitly lists 'Prove Schnirelmann's theorem' so the theorem itself is not yet formalized.

annotations.label: Schnirelmann's bound

anchor.snippet: Schnirelmann himself obtained

annotations.note: Schnirelmann's explicit numeric bound is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ramaré's theorem (1995)

anchor.snippet: who in 1995 showed that every even number n ≥ 4 is in fact the sum of at most 6 primes

annotations.note: Ramaré's six-primes theorem is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Helfgott consequence: sum of at most 4 primes

anchor.snippet: which directly implies that every even number n ≥ 4 is the sum of at most 4 primes

annotations.note: No Helfgott-related theorem appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hardy–Littlewood (1924, under GRH)

anchor.snippet: under the assumption of the generalized Riemann hypothesis that the number of even numbers up to

annotations.note: The Hardy–Littlewood conditional density result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rényi's theorem (1948)

anchor.snippet: every sufficiently large even number can be written as the sum of a prime and an almost prime

annotations.note: Rényi's prime-plus-almost-prime theorem is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chen's theorem (1973)

anchor.snippet: every sufficiently large even number can be written as the sum of either two primes, or a prime and a semiprime

annotations.note: Chen's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Montgomery–Vaughan (1975)

anchor.snippet: there exist positive constants c and C such that for all sufficiently large numbers

annotations.note: Montgomery–Vaughan's exceptional-set estimate is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linnik's theorem (1951)

anchor.snippet: proved the existence of a constant K such that every sufficiently large even number is the sum of two primes and at most K powers of

annotations.note: Linnik's two-primes-plus-powers-of-two theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pintz–Ruzsa (2020): K = 8

anchor.snippet: János Pintz and Imre Ruzsa found in 2020 that

annotations.note: The Pintz–Ruzsa K=8 result is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heath-Brown–Schlage-Puchta (2002, under GRH): K = 7

anchor.snippet: also works, as shown by Roger Heath-Brown and Jan-Christoph Schlage-Puchta

annotations.note: The conditional Heath-Brown–Schlage-Puchta refinement is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weak conjecture implied by Goldbach

anchor.snippet: The weak conjecture is implied by the Goldbach conjecture, as if

annotations.note: The implication between binary and ternary Goldbach is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pipping's verification (1938)

anchor.snippet: Nils Pipping laboriously verified the conjecture up to

annotations.note: Historical computational verifications are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Oliveira e Silva computation

anchor.snippet: T. Oliveira e Silva ran a distributed computer search that has verified the conjecture

annotations.note: Oliveira e Silva's distributed verification is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smallest number not a sum of two primes with one < 9781

anchor.snippet: is the smallest number that cannot be written as a sum of two primes where one is smaller than

annotations.note: This specific computational counterexample statistic is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong/binary Goldbach conjecture

anchor.snippet: It is also known as the " strong ", "even", or "binary" Goldbach conjecture

annotations.note: No definition of the strong/binary Goldbach conjecture exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Goldbach's weak (ternary) conjecture

anchor.snippet: A weaker form of the second modern statement, known as

annotations.note: The ternary Goldbach conjecture is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hardy–Littlewood asymptotic for c primes

anchor.snippet: the number of representations of a large integer n as the sum of c primes

annotations.note: The Hardy–Littlewood representation asymptotic is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hardy–Littlewood twin prime constant

anchor.snippet: where Π 2 is Hardy–Littlewood's twin prime constant

annotations.note: The twin prime constant Π₂ has no definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extended Goldbach conjecture

anchor.snippet: This is sometimes known as the extended Goldbach conjecture

annotations.note: The extended (quantitative) Goldbach conjecture is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Goldbach partition function

anchor.snippet: The Goldbach partition function associates to each even integer the number of ways it can be decomposed into a sum of two primes

annotations.note: The Goldbach partition function is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pillai sequence

anchor.snippet: The Pillai sequence tracks the numbers requiring the largest number of primes in their greedy representations

annotations.note: Grep finds no Pillai sequence in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lagrange's four-square theorem

anchor.snippet: Lagrange proved that every positive integer is the sum of four squares

annotations.note: Nat.sum_four_squares in NumberTheory/SumFourSquares.lean states ∃ a b c d, a^2 + b^2 + c^2 + d^2 = n.

annotations.label: Lemoine's (Levy's) conjecture

anchor.snippet: Every large odd number ( n > 5 ) is the sum of a prime and the double of a prime

annotations.note: Lemoine/Levy's conjecture is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Goldbach for practical numbers (Melfi 1996)

anchor.snippet: every even number is a sum of two practical numbers

annotations.note: Practical numbers are not defined in Mathlib (no grep hits).

annotations.label: Dubner's twin-prime strengthening

anchor.snippet: every even integer greater than 4208 is the sum of two twin primes

annotations.note: Dubner's twin-prime strengthening is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 27-state Turing machine and BB(27)

anchor.snippet: There is a 27-state Turing machine that halts if and only if Goldbach's conjecture is false

annotations.note: This Busy Beaver / Turing machine result is not formalized in Mathlib.
